topic: NMP deceleration in Siberian sector — c5 verify-patch run
change_summary: Decider self-applied all three c4 holes prior to the queue push (queue_id 313 pushed 2026-05-08T09:19:51Z; ISS-1878 fixed_at 2026-05-08T09:18:29Z by decider-self-apply): (Hole-1) sector-bound clause tightened from 'sector-bound vs uniformly distributed' to 'dome-DERIVED prediction... both numbers adopted from NOAA pole-position record'; (Hole-2) Livermore citation narrowed to mechanism + 1990s–2010s acceleration phase, NOAA WMM2025 release notes added as deceleration-era source, with explicit 'same flux-lobe rebalancing model accounts for both phases' qualifier; (Hole-3) Section 6.5 cross-ref expanded to Sections 6.5–6.6. c5 verifies the patches and re-attacks for any new defender wedges.
our_argument_summary: WIN-059 remains Std Model Explains: dome's NMP drift prediction (Kill-Shot Test 6) shows 39.9% error and only PASSes via tolerance widening to 50% from a tighter prior; dome H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is axially symmetric and cannot derive sector-specific behavior; recent deceleration is explained by Livermore-2020 flux-lobe dynamics with the post-2019 phase documented in NOAA WMM2025; same NOAA NP.xy data is reused across WIN-006/007/036/043/059/063 to inflate confirmation count. Post-c4 patch, detail_verdict_text now closes the longitude-tracks-the-sector counterattack by reframing as dome-derived-vs-NOAA-adopted; Livermore citation now correctly attributes acceleration vs deceleration phases; section cross-ref now correctly spans both 6.5 (tolerance-gaming) and 6.6 (asymmetric-scorecard).

patch_verification.c4_hole_1_evidence: Direct substring check on current data/wins.json confirms: old awkward phrase 'sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed' is REMOVED; new phrase 'dome-DERIVED prediction that deceleration would be concentrated in any particular sector' is PRESENT; 'adopted from NOAA' framing is PRESENT. The patch reframes from a contestable sector-vs-distribution dichotomy (where defender could wedge in via the longitude scalar 138.057°E lying IN the Siberian sector) to a dome-derived-vs-data-adopted distinction (where the structural axial-symmetry critique is preserved by detail_evidence and the post-hoc/derives_from_dome=false tags). Patch quality: clean. The recommendation's explicit connecting clause about 'axially symmetric H(r) field has no azimuthal degree of freedom' was not inserted into detail_verdict_text but is already present in detail_evidence ('the dome's field equation H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is axially symmetric — it predicts the same field at all azimuths'), so the structural argument remains visible to the reader.
patch_verification.c4_hole_2_evidence: Direct substring check confirms: 'flux-lobe mechanism' narrowing PRESENT, '1990s–2010s acceleration phase' attribution PRESENT, 'post-2019 deceleration' era source PRESENT, 'WMM2025 release notes' link PRESENT, 'accounts for both phases' qualifier PRESENT. The c4 recommendation (a)+(b) hybrid form was applied: Livermore is now cited explicitly for mechanism + acceleration era, NOAA WMM2025 release notes added for the deceleration-era observation, and the explicit 'the same flux-lobe rebalancing model accounts for both phases' qualifier preempts the era-mismatch attack. ISS-1878 fixed_at 2026-05-08T09:18:29Z by decider-self-apply, verified_at 2026-05-08T11:05:09Z. Patch quality: clean.
patch_verification.c4_hole_3_evidence: Direct substring check confirms: '(Section 6.5)' alone is REMOVED; 'Sections 6.5' (followed by –6.6) is PRESENT. Verified against current data/sections.json:part6 — 6.5 is 'The 4 Acknowledged Failures — 3 Refined, 1 Falsified, and a 5th That Doesn\'t Count' (tolerance-gaming via refinement) and 6.6 is 'The Accuracy Claim' (asymmetric-scorecard). The current 'tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard pattern' framing now correctly references both. Patch quality: clean.
patch_verification.new_holes_introduced_by_patch: none — see hole audit in deeper_analysis

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209-210 — Domain: NMP Longitude (Predicted 139.3°E, Observed 138.057°E, Tolerance 5.0%, Status PASS)

raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 — Domain: NMP Drift Rate (Predicted/Observed adaptive, Tolerance 50.0%, Status PASS at 39.9% error). Note: 'Adaptive tolerance — originally tighter, widened when predictions missed'
raw-text/08-live-power.txt:293 — Author's own 'TOLERANCE GAMING' acknowledgment for NMP Drift Rate (verified at line 293 in current raw-text via direct sed inspection)
raw-text/05-model.txt — H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) functional form (no azimuthal terms; axial symmetry retained from c3/c4)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c4. Domain 3 evaluates rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)). For 2026 → 22.81 km/yr predicted; observed pole-speed ~38 km/yr (NOAA WMM2025); |22.81−38|/38 = 0.399 (matches dashboard's 39.9% error). Domain NMP Longitude is a single-scalar comparison (predicted 139.3°E vs observed 138.057°E) at 5.0% tolerance. The c4-c5 patch is purely textual; no new code paths or formulas were introduced.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Domain 3 is computed from a 2-parameter exponential fit (constants 55 and 0.08); Domain NMP Longitude is a hardcoded single-point scalar comparison. The dashboard labels Domain 3 'adaptive' (tolerance widened post-misses). No dedicated monitoring entry exists for the WIN-059 sector-specific qualifier. Together the two domains monitor (a) deceleration rate and (b) longitude position — both adopted from NOAA NP.xy — but they do not jointly constitute a dome-DERIVED prediction that deceleration concentrates in any particular azimuthal sector.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy / WMM2025 (same dataset reused across WIN-006/007/036/043/063)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged. (1) Tolerance widened to 50% from an originally tighter (unspecified) value — author's own dashboard note. (2) Falsification criterion requires error >50% for THREE consecutive years (temporal escape hatch). (3) 'Siberian sector' is geographically undefined, allowing retroactive boundary redrawing. The c4-c5 patch did not introduce or remove any escape clauses.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The c3 contradiction (detail_evidence citing Domain 3's 39.9% while detail_verdict said no monitoring entry existed) is fully closed since c4. Current detail_verdict_text accurately describes Domain 3 as tracking the scalar drift rate (currently 39.9% off) and Domain NMP Longitude as tracking a single position scalar (138.057°E vs 139.3°E) — both factually verified against raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209-210 and :256-258.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-059's specific claim is sector-bound deceleration. Domain 3 (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:60-65, 256-258) monitors a scalar deceleration rate but does not decompose by azimuth. Domain NMP Longitude (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:209) monitors one longitude scalar at 5% tolerance but is a single-position estimate, not a deceleration mechanism. Neither monitors the WIN's sector-bound interpretation as a dome-derived prediction. Tag 'none' remains consistent with current evidence text and reflects the new dome-DERIVED-vs-NOAA-adopted framing in detail_verdict_text.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: NMP deceleration is documented by NOAA WMM2020/WMM2025 updates and explained mechanistically by Livermore et al. 2020 (flux-lobe rebalancing on the core-mantle boundary; verified DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9). The dome renames the same observational pattern as 'aetheric damping' without altering the numerical curve. The c4 patch correctly attributes the flux-lobe mechanism to Livermore for both the acceleration and deceleration eras.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Livermore 2020 (Nature Geoscience) was published May 2020 — predates the dome's V51 WIN list. NOAA out-of-cycle WMM2020 update had already documented the speed change. The dome's curve-fit formula adopts the post-2020 deceleration trend retroactively. The new detail_verdict_text language 'both numbers are adopted from NOAA's pole-position record after the fact' is verbatim post-hoc framing.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) is a 2-parameter exponential fit to NOAA NP.xy data; the dome's own dashboard labels it 'adaptive.' The dome's H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) field is axially symmetric and has no azimuthal degree of freedom from which a Siberian-sector preference could be derived. 'Siberian sector' is borrowed terminology from Livermore 2020's title.

stronger_arguments.description: GAP WIDENS OVER TIME (carried from c3/c4 — still un-incorporated into WIN-059 detail). Dome formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) projects 22.81 km/yr (2026), 21.06 (2027), 19.44 (2028). Observed pole speed remains ~35–40 km/yr. The dome's curve has the wrong slope, not just a passable offset: by 2028 the dome predicts roughly half the observed rate. Promoting 'gap widens with time' framing into WIN-059 itself would strengthen the kill-shot connection. Could be added to detail_evidence as a one-clause projection: 'projected to widen to ~50% under-prediction by 2028 if observed rates persist.'

WIN OVER-CLAIMS WHAT DOME FORMULA SUPPORTS (new c5 sharpening of c4's null-azimuth bind). The dome's deceleration formula rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) is sector-AGNOSTIC by construction — it predicts a single global rate with no azimuthal dependency. But the WIN's claim is 'NMP deceleration Siberian.' So the WIN itself over-claims relative to the dome's own formula: the formula doesn't support sector-specificity, the WIN does. This is a structural critique sharper than 'axial symmetry has no azimuthal terms' — it's a direct internal-inconsistency point. Detail_evidence could add: 'even setting axial symmetry aside, the dome's deceleration formula is sector-agnostic by construction — predicting one global rate — yet the WIN claims sector-bound deceleration as a confirmed prediction. The formula and the claim are talking past each other.'

kernel_of_truth.description: The NMP HAS decelerated since ~2019 from a ~55 km/yr peak to ~35–40 km/yr today (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258 confirms the dome's tracked 39.9% error against ~38 km/yr observed — confirming the deceleration reality even as the dome's specific formula misses by 39.9%). The trajectory IS toward Siberia (Livermore 2020 verified-DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, paper title contains 'towards Siberia caused by flux lobe elongation'). The exponential functional form rate=55*exp(-0.08*(year-2015)) qualitatively captures deceleration and gives the right sign. The dome correctly identifies that pole behavior changed character around the 2010s.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Four problems remain (unchanged from c4 — verdict carries). (1) Post-hoc adoption: Livermore 2020 (DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9) and NOAA WMM2020 out-of-cycle update predate WIN-059 (data/wins.json:WIN-059.code_analysis.post_hoc=true; c4 review confirmed). (2) The deceleration formula's parameters (55, 0.08) are curve-fit, not derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or aetheric quantities (data/wins.json:WIN-059.code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false). (3) The dome's axially symmetric H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) (raw-text/05-model.txt) has no azimuthal mechanism — 'Siberian sector' is borrowed (relabels_standard=true). (4) The dome's own monitoring (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:256-258) shows 39.9% error and only PASSes because tolerance was widened to 50% from an admittedly tighter prior — making the prediction unfalsifiable by methodological design (raw-text/08-live-power.txt:293 'TOLERANCE GAMING' is the author's own term).

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender accepting our six methodological principles after the c4 patch lands has very little material left. The longitude-tracks-the-sector wedge (c4 Hole-1 residual) is closed by the new 'dome-DERIVED vs adopted from NOAA' framing. The Livermore-era-mismatch wedge (c4 Hole-2) is closed by the explicit 'same flux-lobe rebalancing model accounts for both phases' qualifier and the addition of NOAA WMM2025 release notes as the deceleration-era source. The Section 6.5/6.6 cross-ref (c4 Hole-3) is now correct. Residual defenses: (i) the cluster-data-reuse point (NOAA NP.xy reused across WIN-006/007/036/043/059/063) is structurally true but already acknowledged in the existing text; (ii) the Domain 3 formula IS forward-looking and falsifiable, so a defender could call the post-hoc tag 'unfair' — but the curve-fit nature of the parameters is the legitimate basis for the tag. The strongest residual wedge is rhetorical, not substantive: 'your reviewer keeps moving between Domain 3 (failing at 39.9%) and Domain NMP Longitude (PASS) as if both are the same monitoring artifact for one WIN; pick one.' The current text addresses both because the WIN itself bundles the deceleration claim with a Siberian sector qualifier — but a defender will continue to wedge here. Severity: minor.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: No new edits required to close residual defenses below moderate. The c4 holes are all resolved cleanly. Two stronger_arguments remain unexploited (gap widens over time; WIN over-claims relative to dome formula) — both are sharpenings, not bug fixes. If the decider wants to push the verdict from 'defensible' to 'bulletproof,' incorporating the WIN-over-claims-vs-formula point as a one-clause addition to detail_evidence would close the rhetorical-wedge surface area. Not blocking; 'no_change' is the recommended action.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No factual contradictions detected across the NMP/SAA cluster. Tag heterogeneity (WIN-006/007 monitoring=live_fetch, WIN-036=hardcoded, WIN-043/059/063=none) remains internally defensible by the rule 'live_fetch when monitor.py actually fetches the WIN-claimed value; none when the WIN's specific interpretive claim has no dedicated entry.' The c4-c5 patch did not change WIN-059's monitoring tag (still 'none') and did not introduce inconsistency with sibling WINs. The c4 review's suggestion to add an explicit 'see WIN-007/043 for the same NOAA-NP.xy data reuse pattern' cross-reference remains unexploited but is a stronger_argument, not a hole.

deeper_analysis: Cycle 5 — Verify-Patch Re-Review (queue_id=313, decider self-applied all three c4 holes 2026-05-08T09:18:29Z; queue push 09:19:51Z; ISS-1878 verified by integrity 11:05:09Z) **Triggering event.** Decider self-applied all three c4-flagged holes in the run that closed ISS-1878 and pushed WIN-059 to the priority queue with reason 'Decider self-applied c4 holes 1+2+3 (sector-bound tightening, Livermore citation era fix, Section 6.5→6.5–6.6). Needs c5 verify-patch review.' The pattern matches the c3→c4 verification cycle exactly. **Patch verification — three direct substring checks against current wins.json.** All three pass cleanly: (1) c4 Hole-1 (sector-bound awkwardness, moderate). Old phrase 'sector-bound to Siberia rather than uniformly distributed' → REMOVED. New phrase 'dome-DERIVED prediction that deceleration would be concentrated in any particular sector; both numbers are adopted from NOAA's pole-position record after the fact' → PRESENT. The reframing closes the longitude-tracks-the-sector wedge: a defender can no longer say 'your text concedes I'm monitoring a longitude in the Siberian sector' because the new text isn't about whether the longitude IS in the Siberian sector — it's about whether the dome's formula DERIVES a sector-specific prediction. The structural axial-symmetry critique ('the dome's field equation H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is axially symmetric — it predicts the same field at all azimuths') remains visible to the reader in detail_evidence. Patch quality: high. (2) c4 Hole-2 (Livermore citation precision, moderate; ISS-1878). Five substring checks — 'flux-lobe mechanism', '1990s–2010s acceleration phase', 'post-2019 deceleration', 'WMM2025 release notes', 'accounts for both phases' — all PRESENT. The c4 recommendation (a)+(b) hybrid form was applied: Livermore is now cited explicitly for mechanism plus the acceleration era; NOAA WMM2025 release notes added for the deceleration era; an explicit 'the same flux-lobe rebalancing model accounts for both phases' qualifier preempts the era-mismatch attack entirely. ISS-1878 is now fixed/verified (closed-issues.json:ISS-1878 status=fixed, verified_at=2026-05-08T11:05:09Z, verification_pattern matched on origin/main — independently confirms the patch landed). Patch quality: high. (3) c4 Hole-3 (Section 6.5 cross-ref, minor). Old '(Section 6.5)' alone → REMOVED. New '(Sections 6.5–6.6)' → PRESENT. Verified against data/sections.json:part6 — 6.5 covers tolerance-gaming-via-refinement, 6.6 covers asymmetric-scorecard. The current 'tolerance-gaming and asymmetric scorecard pattern' framing in detail_evidence now correctly references both. Patch quality: high. **New holes introduced by the patch?** None. I attacked the new text from a defender's perspective and found no new wedges introduced by either the Hole-1 reframing or the Hole-2 expansion. The Hole-2 expansion did add a new outbound URL (NOAA WMM2025 release notes) which is a surface-link not deeply verified — flagged in citations_unchecked but not as a hole; tinker/social pass on outbound link health would catch it if the URL ever goes stale. **Adversarial residual scan.** Two stronger_arguments remain unexploited (gap widens over time; WIN claim over-states what the dome formula supports). Neither is a hole — the existing text is internally consistent and structurally defensible. The c4 advocate_mode rated defense_survives=2 pre-patch; post-patch this drops to 1 (the rhetorical Domain-3-vs-Domain-NMP-Longitude wedge), and the residual defense is rhetorical not substantive. 'No_change' is the recommended action. **Verdict assessment.** 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold. The c4-c5 patch did not change verdict-relevant facts. The dome's NMP drift prediction is still failing at 39.9%; the dome's geometry is still axially symmetric; the recent deceleration is still explained by standard flux-lobe dynamics with both eras now correctly attributed; the data is still NOAA NP.xy reused across the cluster. No verdict change recommended. **Tag stability.** All four code_analysis tags unchanged from c4 (monitoring=none, relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false). The c4-c5 patch was textual; no tag-relevant code paths or formulas changed. **Counter-perspective (per user instruction to consider the other side).** A fair-minded dome defender accepting our methodology has lost most of their wedges with the c4 patch landing. The strongest remaining defense is rhetorical: 'your reviewer bundles a failing Domain 3 with a passing Domain NMP Longitude under one WIN, treating both as evidence against a sector-deceleration claim that the dome's own formula doesn't even attempt to predict.' But this defense actually undermines the dome more than the reviewer — if the dome's own formula doesn't support sector-specificity, the WIN is over-claiming what the formula can deliver. We could capitalize on this by adding the c5 stronger_argument ('WIN over-claims relative to dome formula') as a one-clause addition to detail_evidence — but it's not necessary. The existing text is defensible. **Priority for decider.** No new patches required. WIN-059 is in steady state for this revision cycle. If the decider wants to harden further, the two stronger_arguments are queue-ready material for a future EXP. Otherwise, this WIN can be popped from the queue and not re-pushed for a normal cycle. **queue_id assertion (PROP-009).** queue_id set to 313, queue_pushed_at set to 2026-05-08T09:19:51Z, copied verbatim from priority-queue.json[queue][0]. Single queue item this run; matches queue item; STOP after.
